sectionOrderRules: Hero must show the business type or creator role and a clear primary CTA to view work or make contact.
Portfolio preview must appear early on the page and use real project imagery rather than generic stock-like filler.
Specialties should name concrete assignment types or niches, such as portraits, weddings, commercial photo or brand video.
Trust elements like reviews or process summary should support the visual work, not overshadow it.
The final CTA should invite a booking inquiry or project conversation without promising availability that is not confirmed.
Case list items must be concrete and grouped by project, shoot or production rather than shown as unexplained random images.
Each case should help the visitor understand style, context, or assignment type.
If reviews are shown, they should appear after enough work samples have established visual credibility.
Contact CTA should invite the visitor to ask about a similar assignment or discuss their own brief.
Gallery should prioritize image density and visual flow over long paragraphs.
Images should be relevant to the offered work and not duplicate the same selection too heavily from the portfolio page.
Intro copy should explain what kind of material the visitor is seeing, for example portraits, weddings, products or video stills.
About story must be personal, specific and grounded in the creator's actual background or way of working.
Working style should explain collaboration, preparation or delivery in practical terms.
If a team section is used, it should only present real collaborators or crew roles.
Contact page must provide real contact details available in the dossier, without placeholder text.
Contact form should help the visitor describe date, type of assignment, location or desired deliverables when relevant.
Map should only be shown when location is meaningful for studio visits or local presence.
If booking CTA is present, it must support the form rather than compete with it.
